About Me

Fortune Meya

CS Student  ·  Backend Engineer  ·  San Diego
⌥ github.com/FortuneMeya ✉ [email protected]

I'm Fortune, a third-year Computer Science student at California State University, Long Beach, originally from San Diego. I got into programming because I wanted to understand how things actually work under the hood. Once I started, I couldn't stop.

I've had the chance to work as a Backend Engineering Intern at OEE Intellisuite, where I built real-time data tracking systems, optimized APIs, and shipped production code alongside experienced engineers. That experience made it clear that this is exactly what I want to keep doing.

Right now I'm focused on backend systems and distributed architecture. I'm drawn to the kind of problems that live beneath the surface: concurrency, state management, how services communicate. Outside of code, I'm at the gym, reading, watching films, and exploring the city.

I'm actively looking for Summer 2026 internship opportunities. If you're building something real, I want to be part of it.

Current Interests
🎬 Movies 🎵 Electronic / Crystal Castles 🏀 Basketball 🐍 Python 🏋 Gym 📚 Reading

My Life
Journal

Life outside
the terminal.

03/2025

San Diego → New York City

Flew out to NYC with my sister. First time on the East Coast. The city hits different when you're actually in it. The scale, the noise, the energy at night. Times Square felt like something out of a movie. We just walked around, took it all in. One of those trips that reminds you there's a lot of world out there.

2025

Getting Into Reading

Started making time to read outside the screen. Currently working through Reincarnation Blues by Michael Poore. It follows a soul trying to reach perfection across ten thousand lifetimes. It's strange, funny, and unexpectedly deep. Something about sitting outside with a book and no notifications hits different.

2025

Walking the City With a Friend

Grabbed boba and spent the afternoon just walking around with a friend. No real destination. Just moving through the city, talking. Sometimes the best days are the ones with nothing planned.
